featuredPost.title: Things to Do in Nairobi  – Wildlife, Culture & Hidden Experiences
featuredPost.excerpt: Nairobi is a vibrant city where urban energy meets wildlife, rich culture, and hidden experiences waiting to be explored.

content.subheading: Things to Do in Nairobi  – Wildlife, Culture & Hidden Experiences
content.paragraphs1: From immersive tea farm walks and ebike Tours to destination dining, wellness retreats, and family-friendly outdoor experiences, Tigoni has become one of the most rewarding places to visit near Nairobi. Below is a curated guide to the best things to do in Tigoni.
content.closing1:
Tea Farm Walks & Tea Experiences in Tigoni

Tigoni is the birthplace of Kenya’s tea industry, with tea first grown here experimentally in 1903 and the first commercial estate established in 1910. Walking through these estates is both scenic and deeply historic.

Ravenswood Farm offers serene walks through expansive tea fields and indigenous trees, making it ideal for slow nature walks, photography, and dog-friendly strolls. The quiet paths and elevated views give visitors a true sense of Tigoni’s agricultural heritage.

Kiambethu Tea Farm, one of Kenya’s oldest tea farms, provides guided tea experiences that combine plantation walks, tea education, and tastings. Visitors learn how tea is grown, harvested, and processed while enjoying panoramic views of the surrounding countryside.

Gathoni Tea Farm is known for its purple tea and small-scale, sustainable approach. Tours here feel personal and educational, offering insight into modern tea innovation while remaining rooted in Tigoni’s traditions.

Little Falls Tea Garden combines tea gardens with riverside scenery and gentle walking trails, making it a peaceful option for visitors looking to blend nature, water features, and tea landscapes.

eBike Tours in Tigoni

One of the most exciting and accessible ways to explore Tigoni is through a guided eBike tour. With the help of electric-assisted bicycles, visitors can cover more ground comfortably while still enjoying the fresh air, scenic views, and quiet countryside that define the region. eBike tours are suitable for all fitness levels, making them ideal for beginners, families with older children, couples, and groups.

eBike routes in Tigoni wind through rolling tea plantations, forest paths, rural backroads, and scenic viewpoints, offering a deeper look into the landscape beyond what you can see by car. The electric boost makes it easy to tackle Tigoni’s hills, allowing riders to focus on the experience rather than the effort.

Nature Trails & Outdoor Walks

Tigoni’s elevation—approximately 2,100 metres above sea level—creates a cool climate and lush environment that is perfect for walking, hiking, and trail exploration.

The Brackenhurst Forest Trail is one of the most popular nature walks in the area. It winds through indigenous forest, offering shaded paths, birdsong, and a tranquil atmosphere ideal for nature lovers and beginner hikers.

The Ravenswood Trail runs alongside tea fields and forest edges, offering open views and gentle terrain. It is especially popular for relaxed hikes, dog walks, and sunset strolls.

Food & Cafés in Tigoni

Tigoni has quietly become one of Kenya’s most exciting countryside food destinations, offering everything from farm-to-table cafés to refined fusion dining.

The Fig & Olive Restaurant. This iconic Tigoni Cafe is loved for its relaxed elegance, dog-friendly policy, and family appeal. Its children’s section, Amuse, allows kids to paint and create while adults enjoy long lunches by the fireplace or in the garden.

Como offers high-end Kenyan fusion cuisine in a stylish outdoor setting. Created by Swiss-trained chef Stephanie Kiragu, it is ideal for special lunches, refined dinners, and culinary-focused visitors.

Wellness & Spas

For visitors seeking rest and restoration, Tigoni offers a slower, more intentional approach to wellness.

Serenity Spa Tigoni is tucked away in the highlands, offering massages, spa treatments, and wellness experiences surrounded by nature. It is an ideal way to unwind after hiking, cycling, or a full day of exploration.

Why Visit Tigoni?

Tigoni is not a town in the traditional sense—it is a region, shaped by tea, history, landscape, and community. Its cool climate, rolling hills, and understated luxury make it one of Kenya’s most rewarding destinations for slow travel, nature, food, and wellness.

Whether you’re planning a day trip, a romantic escape, a family outing, or a curated multi-activity itinerary, Tigoni offers depth, beauty, and calm—just a short drive from Nairobi.
paragraphs: Nairobi is one of the few cities in the world where you can wake up to the sounds of a modern capital and, within minutes, find yourself among lions, giraffes, and endless savannah. But beyond its famous wildlife lies something deeper—a layered city where culture, history, and countryside experiences quietly shape the journey.
If you’re planning a visit, Nairobi offers more than just a checklist of attractions. It offers contrasts: urban energy and stillness, conservation and community, movement and reflection.
closing: Best Things to Do Near Nairobi (Top Experiences You Shouldn’t Miss)
Tea Farm Tours in Tigoni 🍃 (The Most Underrated Experience)
Tigoni
Just 40 minutes from Nairobi, Tigoni feels like stepping into another world—rolling green hills, cool air, and tea plantations stretching endlessly across the landscape.
A guided tea farm tour here is not just a walk—it’s a story unfolding.

You begin slowly, walking through rows of carefully pruned tea bushes, as your guide explains how Kenya became one of the world’s leading tea producers. You learn about the lifecycle of tea—from planting to hand-picking, to processing methods that determine flavor and quality.

But what makes Tigoni unique is how immersive the experience feels. It’s quiet. You hear birds, wind, and distant water flowing. In some farms, the path leads you to small waterfalls or dams hidden within the estate.

The experience often ends with a tea tasting session—sometimes elevated into a 3-course farm-style meal, where you taste tea the way it was meant to be experienced: fresh, aromatic, and deeply connected to the land.
👉 This is not just an activity—it’s a reset from the city.
E-Bike Tours in Tigoni 🚴‍♂️ (Adventure Meets Serenity)
If the tea farm tour is slow and reflective, the e-bike experience is its dynamic counterpart.

Starting at Ravenswood Farm, you’re introduced to your guide, fitted with a helmet, and given a quick orientation. The e-bike itself is what makes the experience accessible—pedal-assisted, allowing anyone to ride through hills effortlessly.
As you move through Tigoni, the landscape opens up:

• Endless tea fields
  • Narrow countryside paths
  • Hidden forest sections
  • Occasional wildlife sightings
About halfway through, there’s usually a stop at Fig & Olive—a rustic farm restaurant where time seems to pause. From there, the ride continues, often with stops at scenic viewpoints or waterfalls.
What makes this experience powerful is the combination of:

• Movement (you’re actively exploring)
  • Learning (guided storytelling about tea and the region)
  • Stillness (moments where you just take in the view)
👉 It’s one of the most unique outdoor experiences near Nairobi.
Wildlife & Nature Experiences (Nairobi’s Global Identity)
Nairobi National Park 🦁
Nairobi National Park
Few places in the world offer what Nairobi does—a full safari experience within a capital city.
Here, you can see:

• Lions resting in the grasslands
  • Rhinos grazing with the skyline behind them
  • Giraffes moving across open plains
The contrast is surreal—wildlife framed by distant skyscrapers.
Game drives are best done early morning or late afternoon, when animals are most active. For many visitors, this becomes the highlight of their time in Nairobi.
Sheldrick Elephant Orphanage 🐘
David Sheldrick Wildlife Trust
This is where conservation becomes personal.
At the orphanage, rescued baby elephants—often victims of poaching or habitat loss—are cared for before being reintroduced into the wild.

During visiting hours, you watch them being fed, playing in mud, and interacting with caretakers. But beyond the moment, you leave with an understanding of the deeper work being done to protect Kenya’s wildlife.
Karura Forest 🌿
Karura Forest
Karura offers something Nairobi rarely does—silence.
It’s a vast urban forest with:

• Walking and cycling trails
  • Waterfalls and caves
  • Open green spaces
For residents and visitors alike, it’s a place to slow down.
Ngong Hills ⛰️
Ngong Hills
The Ngong Hills rise just outside Nairobi, offering sweeping views of the Rift Valley.
The hike itself is moderate, but the reward is expansive:
rolling hills, strong winds, and uninterrupted horizons.
It’s less about the destination—and more about the feeling of being above it all.
Culture & Heritage (Understanding Nairobi Beyond the Surface)
Bomas of Kenya 🎭
Bomas of Kenya
Here, Kenya’s cultural diversity comes alive through dance, music, and reconstructed homesteads.
It’s not just performance—it’s preservation.
Nairobi National Museum 🏛️
Nairobi National Museum
For those who want context, the museum offers insight into:

• Kenya’s natural history
  • Archaeological discoveries
  • Cultural evolution
Karen Blixen Museum 📖
Karen Blixen Museum
A quiet, reflective space that tells the story of colonial-era Kenya through the life of the Out of Africa author.
KICC Rooftop 🌆
Kenyatta International Conference Centre
If you want to understand Nairobi visually—this is where you go.
From the rooftop, the entire city unfolds beneath you.
Shopping & Markets (The Pulse of the City)
Maasai Market 🛍️
Maasai Market
More than a market—it’s an atmosphere.
Colorful stalls, handmade crafts, negotiation, laughter—it’s where culture becomes tangible.
Kazuri Beads 🎨
Kazuri Beads
A quieter experience, where craftsmanship and social impact meet.
Village Market 🏬
Village Market
A modern contrast to the markets—structured, global, yet still rooted locally.
